title: What Is Employee-Generated Content? The Ultimate Guide
author: Lewis Gray
notes: Used carefully for market definition of employee-generated content and example formats. Vendor source, so not used for independent performance claims.

title: Employee-generated content is the next big thing for brands
author: Aubree Schaefer
notes: Used for current EGC framing around employee influencers, guardrails, employee collaboration, and B2B applicability. Company source; performance implications should be treated cautiously.

title: The Official Guide to Employee Advocacy
notes: Used for LinkedIn's canonical employee advocacy definition and program framing. Older source; pair with current LinkedIn Help source for product-status caveats.

title: My Company tab and Employee Advocacy tab is no longer available
notes: Used to avoid outdated claims about LinkedIn's native Employee Advocacy tab. LinkedIn says the My Company tab, Employee Advocacy tab, and curator admin role began being discontinued in November 2024.

title: 2025 Edelman-LinkedIn B2B Thought Leadership Impact Report
notes: Used for hidden-buyer behavior, receptiveness to thought leadership, and the role of high-quality expertise in B2B buying groups. Does not directly prove EGC program performance.

title: B2B Buying: How Top CSOs and CMOs Optimize the Journey
notes: Used for B2B buying journey context: nonlinear buying tasks, multiple stakeholders, and the need for digital content that builds confidence and supports buying jobs.

title: How to Grow Your AI Search Visibility With LinkedIn Content
author: Margarita Loktionova
notes: Used for LinkedIn/founder/team-led expertise and AI-search visibility guidance. LinkedIn/Semrush analysis should be treated as platform research, not neutral academic evidence.

title: Helping Brands Deliver Impact With Creators and Stronger Creative
author: LinkedIn Corporate Communications
notes: Used as current platform context for B2B creator partnerships and trusted professional voices. Adjacent to EGC; do not conflate external creator programs with employee-generated content.

title: Sell In Public AI Citation Log
author: Codex
notes: Used only for local SERP/AEO observations about what answer engines and search appeared to cite for the comparison query. Not used as factual evidence about EGC or employee advocacy.

title: Sell In Public SME Notes For EGC vs Advocacy
author: Jeffery Schroeder and Codex
notes: Used for Sell In Public operating guidance, measurement POV, and editorial distinctions that are intentionally framed as POV rather than external research findings.
